title: 1.1 Sub-fields of Biology
description: Comprehensive coverage of all sub-fields of biology including zoology, botany, microbiology, and their sub-disciplines, as well as branches based on structure and life processes, and interrelationships with other sciences.

questions.question: Based on the type of organism it studies, biology is subdivided into which three major branches?
What is zoology?
What is the animal biology sub-discipline that studies reptiles?
What is the animal biology sub-discipline that studies fish?
What is the animal biology sub-discipline that studies mammals?
What is the animal biology sub-discipline that studies birds?
What is the animal biology sub-discipline that studies insects?
Zoology is concerned with which aspects of animal life?
options: Embryonic development to mature adulthood, behavior, interactions, food finding, and genetics
Only classification and naming of animals
questions.correctAnswer: Embryonic development to mature adulthood, behavior, interactions, food finding, and genetics

questions.question: Capra walie (Waliya ibex), one of the endemic wild animals of Ethiopia, is found in which mountains?
What is botany?
Botany deals with which aspects of plants?
options: Structure, properties, biochemical processes, classification, diseases, and interactions with the environment
questions.correctAnswer: Structure, properties, biochemical processes, classification, diseases, and interactions with the environment

questions.question: The principles and findings of botany have provided the base for which applied sciences?
According to the text, plants have long been used as a source of which items?
options: Food, shelter, clothing, medicine, ornament, and tools
questions.correctAnswer: Food, shelter, clothing, medicine, ornament, and tools

questions.question: Through which process do green plants convert energy from the Sun into the chemical energy of food?
What is the second unique and important capability of green plants besides converting solar energy into chemical energy?
options: The formation and release of oxygen as a by-product of photosynthesis
questions.correctAnswer: The formation and release of oxygen as a by-product of photosynthesis

questions.question: Ruta chalepensis L., known as 'Tena Adam' in Amharic, is an example of what?
options: A traditional herbal medicine of Ethiopia
An invasive plant species in Ethiopia
questions.correctAnswer: A traditional herbal medicine of Ethiopia

questions.question: What is microbiology?
options: The study of microscopic organisms or microbes that cannot be seen by the unaided eye
questions.correctAnswer: The study of microscopic organisms or microbes that cannot be seen by the unaided eye

questions.question: Which of the following groups are included in the study of microbiology?
options: Bacteria, archaea, protists, viruses, microscopic algae and fungi
questions.correctAnswer: Bacteria, archaea, protists, viruses, microscopic algae and fungi

questions.question: Bacteriology is the study of which type of microbe?
Mycology is the study of which type of microbe?
Why have the majority of bacterial and other microbial species yet to be identified?
options: They are too dangerous to study
They do not exist in significant numbers
Due to the difficulty of isolating a single microscopic species
They evolve before they can be identified
questions.correctAnswer: Due to the difficulty of isolating a single microscopic species

questions.question: What is helping microbiology, a rapidly expanding field, to constantly identify new species?
options: New technology and developments in the field
questions.correctAnswer: New technology and developments in the field

questions.question: Which branch of biology studies the physical structures and parts of organisms?
How is anatomy primarily carried out according to the text?
options: Through observation of living organisms only
By dissection and the separation of parts
questions.correctAnswer: By dissection and the separation of parts

questions.question: Which branch of biology studies the external form and structure of organisms?
Which branch of biology studies the details of tissue structure?
Parenchyma and connective tissue are examples studied in which branch of biology?
Which branch of biology studies cells?
A plant cell and a nerve cell are examples studied in which branch of biology?
Which branch of biology studies the structure, function, and various aspects of the cell and its components?
Mitochondrion, ribosome, and nucleus are examples studied in which branch of biology?
Which branch of biology studies the structure and function of informational molecules such as DNA and RNA?
What does physiology study?
options: The normal functions of living organisms and their parts
questions.correctAnswer: The normal functions of living organisms and their parts

questions.question: Photosynthesis and digestion are examples of topics studied in which branch of biology?
Which branch of biology studies the embryo from a single-celled zygote to the formation of form and shape?
A blastula is an embryo at an early stage of development consisting of what?
Gastrula is the stage in embryonic development after which stage during which the embryo develops two layers?
Which branch of biology studies the interaction of organisms with their living environment?
Food chain, biomass, and biosphere are examples studied in which branch of biology?
Which branch of biology deals with the identification, nomenclature, and classification of organisms?
According to the text, the biological name of a human is Homo sapiens and it was placed in the animal kingdom. This is an example from which field?
Which branch of biology studies the origin, growth, and structure of organisms of the past?
Fossils of organisms are the primary subject of which branch of biology?
Which branch of biology studies the change in the characteristics of a species over several generations that relies on natural selection?
The beaks of Darwin's finches are an example studied in which branch of biology?
Which branch of biology studies heredity and variation?
The gene concept and Mendel's laws are core topics in which branch of biology?
Which branch of biology studies the origin, evolution, distribution, and future of life in the Universe?
Life on Moon and life on Mars are topics studied in which branch of biology?
According to the text, the structure of the cell membrane involving lipids and proteins is explained by which other science?
The formation of oxyhaemoglobin during transportation of oxygen is explained by which other science?
The absorption and elimination of salts in the excretory system involves acid-base equilibrium to maintain $pH$, which is a concept from which science?
The absorption of sugars, amino acids, fatty acids, water, and salts involves diffusion and osmosis where molecules move into and out of the cell. This is explained by which science?
The conduction of water from root to leaves involves cohesion and adhesion resulting in surface tension and capillary action, which are properties studied in which science?
The electron transport chain, which transfers electrons from electron donors to electron acceptors via redox reactions, is involved in which process?
All metabolic pathways involve chemical change, which shows that biology is related to which other science?
What does biochemistry deal with according to the review questions in the unit?
options: The chemical reactions that take place in living organisms
questions.correctAnswer: The chemical reactions that take place in living organisms

questions.question: What is the study of mixed agriculture called?
The evolutionary history of an organism is known as what?
